qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Expired session token remains usable during auth cache TTL
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets stale cached session authentication in src/Auth.js and src/middlewares.js, while the advisory fixes missing JWT audience enforcement in the Google, Apple, and Facebook auth adapters in src/Adapters/Auth/{google,apple,facebook}.js.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's JWT audience-validation bypass in the Google, Apple, or Facebook authentication adapters.
evidence: The advisory describes identity-token audience validation being skipped when Google/Apple clientId or Facebook appIds are absent, allowing valid JWTs issued for other applications to authenticate.
The only materialized qualifying finding is expired_session_cache_bypass, which demonstrates stale cached session reuse after _Session.expiresAt in src/Auth.js:136-149 and src/middlewares.js:368-372.

Inspection of the advisory fix between tags 9.5.0-alpha.10 and 9.5.0-alpha.11 shows changes in src/Adapters/Auth/google.js, src/Adapters/Auth/apple.js, and src/Adapters/Auth/facebook.js to require clientId/appIds and pass them as JWT audiences.
The qualifying finding does not reference or exercise the social-login adapter code paths, JWT audience claims, or missing clientId/appIds configuration.
